segments.s478-g25: خم آرد is an archaic/perhaps poetic verbal phrase: خم (bend/curve) + آرد (third-person singular present 'brings/makes') yields 'causes to bend' or simply 'bends'. ز بالای او is a prepositional source ('from/from the top of her'), placed before the noun it modifies; سرو بن (literally 'cypress' + 'base/root') names what is bending. Word order is verb + source + thing (poetic inversion).
segments.s478-g26: گلفشان شود uses شود (become) to form an inchoative/passive sense: 'becomes flower-scattering' (i.e., bursts into flowers). چو is the temporal conjunction 'when', and سراید is an old third-person singular present meaning 'utters/sings/composes' (often used for singing or speaking verse); سخن is the object 'speech/words'. So: 'it becomes flowering when she utters words.'
segments.s478-g27: ز introduces source ('from'); دیدار و چهرش ('the seeing/meeting and her face') are the sources named. سخن بگذرد uses بگذرد (literally 'passes') in an impersonal/idiomatic sense 'words/speech arise or are spoken' — the predicate follows the source phrase (poetic word order).
segments.s478-g28: همی is an adverb meaning 'ever/always'; داستان را uses را to mark the direct object ('the tale'). خرد پرورد is a verb phrase with پرورد (archaic present stem of 'to nurture') taking خرد ('wisdom') as its object: '[the tale] ever nurtures wisdom.' The subject is general/impersonal (the tale itself).
segments.s478-g29: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; خامش is an adjective/noun 'silent/silence' and بود is the copula 'is/was' (here present/gnomic). جان is 'soul' (subject of the predicate), شرمست is a predicate adjective 'is ashamed/modest'. و بس ('and enough') is a short concluding clause meaning 'and that suffices.' Note the compact, slightly archaic copular phrasing.
segments.s478-g30: چنو (چنان/چنو) = 'such/like this'; در زمانه is the locative phrase 'in the age/era'; ندیدست is an archaic perfect/negative construction meaning 'has not been seen' (impersonal/passive sense), and کس with the negative yields 'no one.' The whole line means 'No one in this age has seen the like.'
segments.s478-g31: سپهبد is a title/noun placed before its predicates; نژادست is a predicate composed of نژاد ('lineage') + ـست ('is') giving 'is of noble lineage/stock'. و links the coordinate predicate یزدان‌پرست ('God-worshipper'/'pious'). This is a compact copular construction typical of older Persian.
segments.s478-g32: دل at the start names the locus ('heart'); شرم و پرهیز ('shame/modesty and abstinence/restraint') are the qualities, and دارد is the verb 'has/holds'. به دست is a small prepositional phrase literally 'in (his) hand' or 'at hand/under his control' — together: 'he has modesty and restraint in his heart and at hand.' The sequence places the locus first (دل) and the possessed qualities after it, a common poetic order.
segments.s478-g41: «اگر باشد» introduces a conditional (classical Persian uses the present/subjunctive form باشد after اگر to mean 'if he is/has'); «او» is the third-person pronoun 'he' (referring back to the person just mentioned); «سالیان پیش‌گاه» is a compact expression meaning 'for years at the court/audience' (پیش + گاه = پیشگاه, 'the royal presence/audience'), so the whole chunk means 'if he has been for years at court'.
segments.s478-g42: «ز دردی» is an ablative/causal phrase 'from/because of pain'; «نپیچد» is the negative present/subjunctive (prefix ن- + پیچد) used here as a predictive/result clause 'will not writhe / will not be twisted'; «جهاندار شاه» is an appositive epithet 'the world‑holding king' (جهاندار modifying شاه), so the clause means 'the world‑ruling king will not writhe from pain.'
segments.s478-g43: «چهارم» signals the fourth item in a list; «نهان دارم» places نهان ('hidden/secretly') adverbially before دارم ('I have') to mean 'I secretly have/keep'; «از انجمن» uses the preposition از ('from/of/among') with انجمن ('assembly, council') to indicate source or membership—here 'among the assembly/council.'
segments.s478-g44: «یکی» is the indefinite 'a certain' or 'one (of)'; «فیلسوفست» is the archaic/enclitic form of 'فیلسوف است' (the -ست = است), functioning as the predicate 'is a philosopher'; «نزدیک من» is a locative/relational phrase 'near me / close to me.' Together: 'a certain philosopher is close to me.'
segments.s478-g45: «همه بودنیها» is a poetic plural noun phrase 'all the beings / all that exists' (بودنیها = that which is); «بگوید» is the 3rd-person present/subjunctive form often used in classical Persian as a future/predictive 'he tells / he will tell'; «به شاه» marks the indirect object 'to the king.'
segments.s478-g46: «ز» here is the preposition 'from/about/concerning'; «گردنده خورشید» and «رخشنده ماه» are adjectival epithets 'the circling/turning sun' and 'the shining/radiant moon' (گردنده and رخشنده are participial/adjectival forms); «و» links the two celestial epithets. The phrase restricts the content of what he tells the king.
segments.s478-g47: «فرستاده نامور بازگشت» is a simple clause: فرستاده 'the envoy/messenger' modified by the epithet نامور 'famed/renowned', with بازگشت the verb 'returned' (classical past/perfect sense).
segments.s478-g48: This lineive/poetic clause combines several adverbial elements: «پی باره» (پی = 'after/then', باره = 'on an occasion/after a while') gives a temporal 'shortly after / then'; «با باد» 'with the wind' is a simile/means phrase; «انباز» is an obscure/archaic intensifying adjective (here implying swiftness); and «گشت» is a classical third‑person past/preterite meaning 'he went/turned'—so the chunk says he left/returned again swiftly like the wind. (Several of these words are archaic or condensed; the overall sense is 'soon after, with the wind he went/was gone swiftly.')
segments.s478-g1: فرستاده (subject) + آمد is a simple past 'came'. به کردار باد is a prepositional phrase 'in the manner of the wind' (به here used adverbially 'by/in a ... way', کردار = 'manner/conduct', باد = 'wind') — a poetic simile meaning he arrived quickly.
segments.s478-g2: بگفت is a past narration form 'he said / he told' (classical prefixed form بــ + گفت). آنچ is the contracted relative 'that which' = آن‌چه. بشنید is past 'he heard' (reportive past). و links the clauses; نامه بداد is 'gave the letter' (داد 'gave'); the provided lemma بداد does not include the verb sense 'gave', so that sense is marked as unavailable.
segments.s478-g3: سکندر is the proper subject. فرستاده stands in apposition / topic ('the messenger' just mentioned). از introduces the source or cause ('from/because of'), گفت here names the 'words/speech' heard, and رو is the noun 'face' used idiomatically with رو به ... 'to turn the face toward' (the verb 'to turn' is implied by the idiom).
segments.s478-g4: به نزدیک = 'toward/near to' (به as directional 'towards/into', نزدیک = 'near'). آن 'that' points to a definite thing; نامور is an epithet 'renowned/famous'; بازشو is a noun 'opening, gate' — together 'toward that famed opening.'
segments.s478-g5: بگویش is the imperative/jussive 'tell him' (بگو + ـیش = 'to him'). که introduces a subordinate/relative clause 'that/which'. آن چیست is the question/relative 'what that is.' کاندر (ک + اندر) is the old-fashioned locative 'which in/within' and جهان 'the world' completes the relative reference.
segments.s478-g6: کسی را نبود is a negative past 'there was no one' (کسی = 'anyone / here 'no one' because of نبود; را is the definite/contrastive marker/emphatic object particle). آشکار و نهان are paired adverbial/adjectival opposites 'openly and secretly' — the sense is 'there was no one who did not know it, neither openly nor in secret' (the omitted verb 'knew' is understood from context).
segments.s478-g7: بدیدند is simple past 'they saw'. خود is emphatic 'themselves / itself' (stress on the observer). بودنی is a substantive/adjectival form 'the being, that which exists'. هرچ بود 'whatever there was' is a resumptive relative ('هرچ' = 'whatever', followed by the past copula بود). The clause means 'they themselves saw whatever existed.'
segments.s478-g8: سپهر آفرینش is a genitive/nominal phrase 'the heavens of creation' or 'the sky (over) creation'. نخواهد فزود is a classical verbal phrase where نخواهد gives future/will and فزود (from فزاییدن) means 'to increase' — together 'will not / cannot add (more)'. The sense: the heavens will not increase (i.e., add anything more to) creation.
segments.s478-g49: بیامد is a simple past verb (‘he came’). چو is the temporal conjunction ‘when’ linking the arrival to what follows. پیش + name (پیش سکندر) is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘in the presence of / before Sikandar’. بگفت is an archaic past-tense reporting verb (‘he said’), composed of the verbal root گفت with the poetic prefix ب‑/بَ‑.
segments.s478-g50: This is a compact noun phrase and predicate with omitted ezāfe (typical in poetry): دلِ شاهِ گیتی = ‘the heart of the king of the world’. چو is the simile particle ‘like/as’. گل is the image ‘flower’. بر + شگفت here yields a predicative phrase meaning ‘struck with wonder / astonished’; بر is the preposition ‘upon’ used idiomatically with the adjective شگفت (‘wonder’/‘astonishment’).
segments.s478-g51: بدو گفت is inverted word order common in older/narrative Persian: بدو = ‘to him’, گفت here functions as the verb ‘said’ (the annotator’s senses list gives noun senses, but grammatically it is the verb). اگر باشد is the conditional protasis: اگر = ‘if’, باشد is the present/subjunctive form of بودن ‘to be’ (used here as ‘if it is’). این گفته راست = ‘this saying [is] true’ is the content of the conditional; راست is the predicate adjective ‘true’. The main clause is left open (ellipsis) because the sentence continues.
segments.s478-g52: بدین چار چیز is a prepositional phrase: بدین = ‘by/with these’ (ب = preposition + this/demonstrative), چار چیز = ‘four things’. او here is an impersonal/introductory subject (literary) meaning roughly ‘it/there’ or referring back to the proposition; جهان را is the definite object ‘the world’ (را marks the object). بهاست is a fixed predicative compound = ‘it is worth / has value’ (i.e. ‘the world’s value is by these four things’). The whole line asserts that the world’s worth depends on those four things.
segments.s478-g53: چو introduces a temporal/conditional clause ‘when/if’. اینها = ‘these (things)’. فرستد is third-person present/subjunctive form of فرستادن ‘to send’ (in poetry this form carries a future/optative sense: ‘if/when he sends’). به نزدیک من is a prepositional phrase ‘to my proximity / to my presence’ with به + noun followed by the pronoun من.
segments.s478-g54: درخشان شود is a two-word predicate meaning ‘become shining/radiant’: درخشان is an adjective ‘shining’, شود is the subjunctive/auxiliary of شدن ‘to become’ giving a future/potential sense. جان تاریکِ من = ‘my dark soul’ (again the ezāfe is omitted in poetic diction: جانِ تاریکِ من). The clause says the speaker’s gloomy soul will be brightened.
segments.s478-g55: بر و بوم = ‘land and abode/home’ (a paired expression meaning realm and dwelling). او را marks the possession/object ‘his’ (او) and the object marker (را). نکوبم is the first-person singular present/subjunctive/optative of کوبیدن ‘to strike/crush’, here idiomatically ‘I will crush/beat down’ (poetic future). به پای = ‘to/at the foot [of]’ = ‘beneath my feet’ (literal/figurative). The clause expresses violent action against the enemy’s realm.
segments.s478-g56: برین نیکویی = ‘by/with this goodness’ (برین = بر این = ‘by this’ in fused poetic form; نیکویی = ‘goodness/virtuous deed’). باز گردم is a verbal phrase ‘I will return again’: باز = ‘again’, گردم is the first-person subjunctive/present of گشتن/گردیدن used here as ‘I return / I will turn back’. به جای = ‘to [my] place’ i.e. back to his former position or home. The syntax is cause → result: because of that goodness he will return.
segments.s478-g9: بیامد is a past tense verb 'came' placed before its object (a common order in epic Persian). فرستاده را uses the enclitic را as the definite/direct-object marker (فرستاده = 'the messenger'), and نزد شاه is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before the king' specifying the goal/place.
segments.s478-g10: به کردار آتش is a simile/Adverbial phrase introduced by به + noun phrase ('in/with the manner of fire'); بپیمود is the past tense verb 'traversed/spanned' (archaic/poetic form with prefixed ب‑), and راه is the object 'the road/path'. Word order here places the adverbial phrase before the verb and its object.
segments.s478-g11: چنین گفت = 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus'); با کید ('with a stratagem') marks the manner/means of speaking. کاین چار چیز begins a nominal phrase introduced by the demonstrative/relative کاین ('this/that') + شمار 'چار' = 'four' and چیز = 'things'. Note گفت is used as the verb 'said' in context.
segments.s478-g12: This is a relative clause introduced by که 'that' describing the four things: کس را ... نبودست نیز = 'which no one in the world had either.' کس را uses را as a topical/definite marker on 'no one/anyone,' به گیتی = 'in the world,' and نبودست is an older past form 'did not exist/was not' with نیز adding 'also/either.'
segments.s478-g13: همی is a poetically emphatic/continuous particle (here functioning as an emphatic/ongoing marker), شاه خواهد = 'the king wishes/wants' (خواهد used for desire), and که داند = 'that he may know' (subordinating که + verb). The embedded question چیست ('what is it') appears as چی + ست (interrogative + copula) at the clause end.
segments.s478-g14: This clause further explains what is to be known: که introduces the clause 'that ...', نادیدنی is an adjective 'unseen/invisible' as the subject, پاک here functions adverbially/intensifying 'utterly/entirely', نابود 'destroyed', and نیست is the negative copula 'is not.' The order places the intensifier before the adjective ('پاک نابود نیست' = 'is not completely destroyed').
segments.s478-g15: چو بَشنید is a temporal clause 'when he heard' (چو = 'when'), کید آن = 'that plot' (object of شنیدن), ز بیگانه = 'from the stranger' (ز = 'from'), and جای here functions as 'place/source' or 'from that place' — the whole phrase gives source/time: 'when he heard that plot from the stranger.'
segments.s478-g16: بپردخت is a past verb (archaic form of پرداخت) meaning 'he dealt with/attended to/paid (it)' here 'addressed the matter'; و = 'and'; بنشست = 'he sat down'; با رهنمای = 'with the guide/adviser' (با + noun). The clause states two successive actions he took after hearing the news.
segments.s478-g33: دگر جام دارم که پر می‌کنی — Word order is: determiner/quantifier + noun + verb + relative clause. دگر here means “another”; جام is the noun “cup”; دارم is present tense first person singular “I have.” که introduces a relative clause (“that/which”) and پر می‌کنی is a present tense clause ‘you fill (it)’ where پر is the adjective ‘full’ used with کردن (می‌کنی = you make/fill).
segments.s478-g34: وگر آب سر اندرو افگنی — وگر is a conditional connector “and if / but if.” آب is literal “water.” سر اندرو is an older order for سرِ در او / سر اندر او = “on/at its head/top / on it”; اندرو = ‘in/on it.’ افگنی is an older/poetic 2nd-person verb form meaning something like ‘you cast/pour/throw’ (the exact dictionary senses given do not match this verbal use), so the clause means “if you pour water on/into it.”
segments.s478-g35: به ده سال اگر با ندیمان به هم — به ده سال is an adverbial phrase “for ten years” (به + period). اگر is the conditional ‘if.’ با introduces accompaniment ‘with.’ ندیمان is the plural ‘companions/banquet-companions’ (the provided senses do not match that lemma). به هم means ‘together’ (به + هم).
segments.s478-g36: نشیند نگردد می‌از جام کم — نشیند is 3rd-person present/subjunctive of ‘to sit’ (here in the protasis: “if he sits”). نگردد is a negative present/optative 3rd-person of گردیدن/گشتن meaning “does not become / will not turn into.” می‌از جام کم functions as the remainder of the clause: می‌از = the wine/’mey’ of, جام = the cup, کم = ‘less / diminished’; together meaning “there will not be less (taken) from the cup.” Word order is typical older Persian, with predicate and negation placed after the protasis.
segments.s478-g37: همت می‌دهد جام هم آب سرد — همت (direct object) is “resolve/ambition” and می‌دهد is 3rd-person present “gives/instills.” The phrase جام هم آب سرد should be read as a noun phrase “a cup (also) [of] cold water” (جام = cup, هم = ‘also’, آب سرد = cold water). The verb-means-object order is typical: subject is omitted (third-person implicit).
segments.s478-g38: شگفت آنک کمی نگیرد ز خورد — شگفت = ‘wondrous / strange.’ آنک is a relative ‘that/which.’ کمی here means ‘a little’ or ‘only a little.’ نگیرد is negative present/subjunctive of گرفتن ‘to take/seize’ (third person), and ز خورد = ‘from eating/drinking’ (ز = from, خورد as gerundial/nominal form related to خوردن). Together: “It is wondrous that only a little is not taken by drinking/eating.”
segments.s478-g39: سوم آنک دارم یکی نو پزشک — سوم = ‘third’ (ordinal, adverbial). آنک introduces a clause ‘that/which (I have).’ دارم is present first-person ‘I have.’ یکی نو پزشک = ‘a certain new physician’ (یکی = an indefinite ‘one/certain,’ نو = new, پزشک = physician) — adjective follows the numeral/indefinite.
segments.s478-g40: که علت بگوید چو بیند سرشک — که = relative ‘who/that.’ علت is the direct object ‘the cause.’ بگوید here is 3rd-person present/subjunctive (or future/optative in classical style) ‘may tell / will tell.’ چو = temporal conjunction ‘when.’ بیند is 3rd-person present ‘he sees.’ سرشک = ‘teardrop / small bead of fluid’ (object of بیند). The clause means ‘who will explain the cause when he sees the teardrop.’
segments.s478-g17: فرستاده را is the direct object marked by the postposition را; this fronting gives topical/emphatic focus on the envoy. پیش is an adverb/prepositional word meaning “in the presence of / before (them).” بنشاختند is a past 3rd-person-plural verb (classical/poetic form) meaning “they seated/they caused to sit,” so the clause reads literally “they seated the envoy before (them).”
segments.s478-g18: ز هر در is a small prepositional phrase (ز = from/of + هر در = every door/gate) meaning “at every gate” in this context. فراوانش functions adverbially (‘plentifully/abundantly’), with the clitic -ش referring back to the action; بنواختند is past 3rd-plural (‘they played/struck’), here referring to sounding instruments or striking (drums/trumpets) repeatedly.
segments.s478-g19: ازان پس is an adverbial phrase meaning “thereupon / after that.” The sequence فرستاده را شاه گفت places the object (فرستاده را) before the subject (شاه) and verb (گفت): Persian allows this topicalization; گفت is the past verb ‘said’ (here introducing reported speech).
segments.s478-g20: که introduces reported speech (‘that’). من is the first-person subject and دختری دارم is a simple present/past-to-present possession construction (‘I have a daughter’), with دارم as the present/presentive ‘I have.’ اندر نهفت is a classical locative+adjective phrase: اندر = ‘in/within’ and نهفت = ‘hidden/kept concealed,’ postpositive and describing where she is kept.
segments.s478-g21: که‌گر (classical contraction of که گر) = ‘if’ introduces a conditional. بیندش is 3rd-person singular (classical/subjunctive/present-form) meaning ‘sees/beholds her’ (the clitic -ش = ‘her’). آفتاب بلند is noun + postpositive adjective (‘the lofty/high sun’), typical of poetic word order.
segments.s478-g22: شود is the 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive of ‘become’ (used here as the conditional/result: ‘would become’). تیره is the predicate adjective ‘dark,’ and از روی آن = ‘from/on account of that face’ (از = from, روی = face/surface, آن = that). ارجمند is a postpositive adjective meaning ‘noble/esteemed,’ so the clause says the sun would be made dark by that noble face.
segments.s478-g23: کمندست is a compound adjective (کمند ‘snare’ + -ست ‘is/being’) meaning ‘ensnaring’ or ‘is a snare,’ used predicatively to describe the hair. گیسوش is گیسو (hair) + pronominal clitic -ش = ‘her hair.’ همرنگ قیر is a simile/description ‘the same color as tar’ (همرنگ = same-colored, followed by the comparison noun قیر = tar).
segments.s478-g24: همی آید is a classical continuous/habitual verbal phrase (همی marks ongoing/habitual aspect; آید = ‘comes’) meaning ‘there always comes.’ از دو لبش = ‘from her two lips’ (از = from; دو لبش = her two lips). بوی شیر = ‘the scent of milk’ (بوی = scent; شیر = milk), the noun phrase that is said to come from her lips.

sentences.s478-s13: From atop her (or his) height the cypress's stem bows, and it breaks into showers of blossom when she utters speech.
sentences.s478-s14: From the sight of her and from her face words arise; the tale ever nurtures wisdom.
sentences.s478-s15: When she is silent the soul is modest, and that is enough; no one in the age has seen the like.
sentences.s478-s16: The spahbed is of noble stock and God-worshipping; modesty and self-restraint he keeps in heart and in hand.
sentences.s478-s21: If he has spent years at court, the world-ruling king will not writhe from pain.
sentences.s478-s22: Fourth, I secretly keep among the assembly a philosopher close to me.
sentences.s478-s23: He tells the king all things that exist concerning the circling sun and the radiant moon.
sentences.s478-s24: The famed envoy returned; shortly afterward he went back like the wind.
sentences.s478-s1: The messenger came like the wind, said what he had heard, and handed over the letter.
sentences.s478-s2: Alexander, at the messenger's words, turned his face toward that famed opening.
sentences.s478-s3: Tell him what that is, for in the world there was no one who did not know it, openly or secretly.
sentences.s478-s4: They themselves saw the being — whatever there was; the heavens would not add to creation.
sentences.s478-s25: He came; when he stood before Sikandar he said, “The heart of the king of the world is amazed like a flower.”
sentences.s478-s26: He said to him, “If this saying is true…”,
sentences.s478-s27: When he sends these to my presence, my dark soul will become radiant.
sentences.s478-s28: I will crush his land and home beneath my feet; by this good deed I will return again to my place.
sentences.s478-s5: The messenger came before the king; like fire he traversed the road.
sentences.s478-s6: Thus he said, with a stratagem, that these four things which no one in the world had (either).
sentences.s478-s7: The king wished to know what it was — that the unseen is not utterly destroyed.
sentences.s478-s8: When he heard that plot from the stranger, he dealt with it and sat down with his adviser.
sentences.s478-s17: I have another cup that you fill, and if you pour water onto its head...
sentences.s478-s18: Even if he sits together with companions for ten years, there will be no diminution from the cup.
sentences.s478-s19: It instills resolve; the cup also gives cold water — wonderful that a little is not taken by drinking.
sentences.s478-s20: Third, I have a certain new physician who will tell the cause when he sees the teardrop.
sentences.s478-s9: They seated the envoy before them, and at every gate they sounded (their instruments) in abundance.
sentences.s478-s10: After that the king said to the envoy that I have a daughter kept hidden.
sentences.s478-s11: If the lofty sun beheld her, the noble sun would be darkened by that face.
sentences.s478-s12: Her hair is a snare, the color of tar; from her two lips there always comes the scent of milk.
